surface_verification.justification: Python Stop-hook/lib + verifier change with no user-facing web/UI surface and no backend-affects-frontend contract; behavior is fully covered by unit tests (40 focused + full 2660-pass shared suite) plus an end-to-end reproduction probe of the exact 9-item flood (→ 0 items). No dev server / E2E surface applies.

behaviors.behavior: phase_is_engaged: event / iterate-when-complete / in-progress cursor / casing (AC-1,2,3)
behaviors.evidence: test_phase_quality_engagement.py (11 cases incl stale-cursor + casing) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: collect_in_scope_fails: multi-code per phase, filter unengaged, exclude tier-2/pass/skip, latest-per-phase, fail-open (AC-4)
collect excludes provenance=error + source=error masking (review MEDIUM + LOW-1)
S2/S3 SKIP on sentinel/no-exact-entry; file-on-disk→PASS; exact-entry→FAIL/WARN preserved (AC-5,6)
single rolling backlog item with body+launch_payload (AC-7, AC-16 sig-derivable)
idempotent within/across sweeps; convergence to one item (AC-8, AC-15)
refresh on changed FAIL set; dismiss stale-sig (AC-9)
legacy {phase}:{code} items untouched; dismissed backlog re-fires (AC-11)
unengaged-only / no-fails → no item, no error (AC-13 best-effort)
events log read via SSOT accessor (no raw join)
hook delegates to emit_phase_quality_backlog; old per-FAIL fn removed
behaviors.evidence: import smoke (hook main present, _emit_tier1_fails_to_triage gone) + test_audit_phase_quality.py + test_hook_output_schema_compliance.py PASSED

behaviors.behavior: end-to-end flood elimination on the real framework-repo scenario (9 FAILs → 0 items)
behaviors.evidence: Step 7.5 reproduction probe: collect_in_scope_fails=0, emit appended=0; no-over-suppression probe surfaces engaged iterate:W2

behaviors.behavior: docs hooks-and-pipeline.md describes the backlog action-unit shape (AC-14)
behaviors.reason: documentation prose, not executable behavior; the producer behavior it describes is covered by the tests above

degraded.detail: Full shared suite: 2660 passed, 14 skipped, 2 FAILURES that are PRE-EXISTING and unrelated to this iterate — confirmed by re-running on the clean origin/main base with this work stashed: test_github_triage.py::test_failed_fetch_does_not_resolve_items (github producer, order-dependent) and test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact.py::test_every_arch_impact_drop_has_architecture_md_entry (repo-hygiene: main-tree decision-drops not reflected in architecture.md). This iterate touches only shared/scripts/lib/phase_quality + tools/verifiers/spec_checks + their tests; neither failing test references those files.

External LLM iterate review (gemini-3.1-pro + gpt-5.4 via OpenRouter) ran on the plan and returned 12 findings; dispositions folded into the spec (fail-open, S2/S3 file-on-disk guard, sig-derivable body, concurrency convergence, consumer audit). The internal code-reviewer subagent then reviewed the diff: 1 MEDIUM (provenance=error pollution) + 3 LOW (error-source masking, stale bloat baseline, code fallback) — all fixed.
